domain: Bhutanese gho hemchu (Bhutan national dress; kera belt folded 3x creating private pouch for items; access requires ritual unfolding protocol; tuck-direction encodes social status)
specific_mechanism: Attention-mask gating of designated context tokens accessible only by cryptographically-signed unfold-trigger verified via canary token in the system prompt header.
llm_application: GHO-HEMCHU-POCKET: a context-gating design where designated 'hemchu pocket' tokens in the context window are masked from default attention (attention_mask[pocket_indices]=0 by default). Access requires a privileged 'unfold' signal in the system prompt that is verified by a canary token check (cryptographic hash of pocket index + role token). Legitimate elevated-privilege instructions verify the canary and toggle the attention mask to expose pocket tokens during one specific decode step, then re-mask. Differs from regular scratchpad (always-accessible), Hidden Scratchpad (no cryptographic verification), Memory Sparse Attention routing keys (retrieval not gating), SP-FT parameter-level privacy, Instruction Hierarchy (privilege levels but not token-mask gating) by combining (a) per-token attention-mask gating, (b) cryptographic-canary unfold-trigger, (c) one-step mask-toggle reveal.
novelty_claim: Hidden Scratchpad, Engineering Trustworthy LM Agents with Scratchpads, ICLR 2026 scratchpad already cover private deliberation. Canary-token verification (Hidden Markov / Constitutional AI) exists. GHO-HEMCHU-POCKET recombines attention-mask + canary-verification + one-step toggle. Recombination of existing components.
